checks.a_analysis: All text in the output can be traced back to the source PDF. The headings and content blocks correspond exactly to the slides in the presentation.

The URLs provided in the output match the source document exactly. Page 2 contains 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc-annual-report-FY-20-21' and Page 13 contains 'www.nconemap.gov/pages/feedback'. No other contact info is present.
All 13 slides from the source PDF are represented as sections in the JSON output, meaning no pages were dropped.

The content from the first page's text blocks was appropriately grouped into a structured table format in the JSON. No rows are missing; all presentation details (Governor, Secretary, Chair, etc.) are included.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output captures the main content of each slide. While some minor descriptive sub-text on Page 2 (like 'Meeting Schedule, Agendas...') is only summarized in the image alt text, the primary information (headings and links) is preserved. This is acceptable for converting visual slide content into structured text.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the JSON matches the source text accurately. There are no changes to names, dates, or key phrases that would alter the information conveyed.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON represents each content block exactly once, mirroring the source document's structure across its 13 pages. No paragraphs, list items, or headings are repeated inappropriately.

The JSON uses a flat and consistent section title structure for each page/major slide title (e.g., '2020-2021 GICC Annual Report', 'What We're Doing', 'Outline'). This accurately reflects the presentation-style layout of the source. No heading levels are skipped.

Only one table is defined, located in the first section. This table captures the 'Presented to' sidebar from Page 1. While the source uses a layout block rather than a formal data table, the conversion into a header/data cell structure is logically sound and preserves the grouping of the presentation information.

The reading order follows the sequential page order (1-13). Within pages, it correctly prioritizes titles, then primary body content (paragraphs or lists), then footer/logo elements. On Page 1, it reads the main title and authors before the presentation sidebar, which is a logical flow.

The 'Outline' on Page 3 is correctly converted into a nested unordered list, preserving the relationship between 'Accomplishments' and its sub-items. The bulleted visual blocks on Pages 4-7 are also appropriately converted into lists.
Hyperlinks present in the source (Page 2 and Page 13) are correctly captured in the JSON as paragraph text. No external links are missing or fabricated.

checks.a_analysis: The 'What We're Doing' section on page 2 of the source PDF is organized as a complex grid of seven distinct tiles, each with an icon and text. In the HTML output, this section is reduced to a single-item layout, omitting six of the seven tiles and losing the original grid-based visual organization. This significantly changes the visual flow and intended presentation of that section.
checks.b_evidence_source: A grid of seven tiles including 'Council Meetings', 'Upcoming GICC Events', 'Committees & Work Groups', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: Only a single entry for 'Annual Report FY 20-21' is visible under the 'What We're Doing' heading.

checks.a_analysis: The converted document includes a table titled 'Report Presentation Details' which correctly structures the 'Presented to' list from the cover page of the PDF. The table is well-formed with visible borders and aligned content.

Throughout the document, multiple meaningful images and icons are replaced by solid green blocks. This includes the category icons on pages 8-12 (map, compass, cloud, etc.) and the final photo on page 13. While the NCDIT logos render correctly, these meaningful categorical graphics are visually absent, replaced by non-descript green rectangles.
checks.b_evidence_source: Icons for 'ADDRESS NC STEERING COMMITTEE' (map icon) and 'WORKING GROUP FOR SEAMLESS PARCELS' (compass icon) on page 8; closing photo on page 13.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML shows solid green blocks in place of these icons and the final photo.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Collaboration for Data Governance and Consistency' through 'Questions?'

checks.a_analysis: A thorough visual scan of the HTML output reveals no instances of duplicated content blocks, paragraphs, or sections.
